L1.area: Python code evaluation and sandboxing infrastructure

L2.description: A sandbox escape vulnerability exists in the restricted Python code execution environment. The security boundary fails to validate return values when dispatching calls through trusted callable references, allowing an attacker to indirectly access unauthorized modules and dangerous system-level functions through attribute chains. This enables arbitrary code execution on the host system, completely bypassing the sandbox restrictions intended to isolate untrusted code.

L3.area: Code execution sandbox's AST evaluation and name resolution pipeline
L3.description: A sandbox escape vulnerability exists in the restricted Python code execution environment. When resolving names that correspond to registered trusted callable references, these callables are returned without wrapping their return values in safety checks. An attacker can exploit this by invoking a trusted callable and then traversing attribute chains on its return value to access unauthorized submodules or dangerous system-level functions, achieving arbitrary code execution on the host.

ground_truth.reason: The sandbox's name resolution logic returns trusted tool functions without safety-wrapping their return values, allowing attackers to access unauthorized modules and dangerous functions through indirect attribute access, enabling arbitrary code execution.
